seo.title: AI API Cost Optimization implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping AI API Cost Optimization projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for reducing LLM API expenses by optimizing model selection, implementing caching layers, and enforcing strict token management protocols before scaling to production.

items.title: Implement a Multi-Model Gateway
items.description: Deploy a proxy like LiteLLM or OpenRouter to programmatically switch between providers without refactoring code.

items.title: Benchmark Against 'Mini' Models
items.description: Verify if tasks like classification, summarization, or extraction can be handled by GPT-4o mini, Gemini Flash, or Claude Haiku instead of flagship models.

items.title: Define Intent-Based Routing Logic
items.description: Implement a lightweight classifier to route high-complexity queries to flagship models and low-complexity queries to cheaper alternatives.

items.title: Configure Automated Fallback Sequences
items.description: Set up logic to attempt requests on a cheaper model first, falling back to a more expensive model only if the output fails validation or safety checks.

items.title: Utilize Local Models for Dev/Test
items.description: Configure Ollama or vLLM for local development and CI/CD testing to eliminate API costs during the build phase.

items.title: Deploy Exact-Match Request Caching
items.description: Implement a Redis-based cache to store and return identical prompt-response pairs for 0 tokens.

items.title: Implement Semantic Caching
items.description: Use a vector database to identify and serve cached responses for prompts that are semantically similar but not identical.

items.title: Enforce Per-User Cache Isolation
items.description: Ensure cache keys include user identifiers to prevent data leakage between different user sessions while maintaining cost savings.

items.title: Configure Cache TTLs
items.description: Set time-to-live values on cached LLM responses to ensure data freshness and prevent stale content delivery.

items.title: Monitor Cache Hit Rates
items.description: Integrate tools like Helicone or Portkey to track the financial impact of your caching layer in real-time.

items.title: Enforce Hard max_tokens Limits
items.description: Set explicit output token limits on every API call to prevent runaway generation and predictable cost ceilings.

items.title: Prune System Prompts
items.description: Audit system messages to remove redundant instructions, filler text, and overly verbose formatting requirements.

items.title: Implement Dynamic Few-Shot Selection
items.description: Retrieve only the most relevant few-shot examples for the current context rather than sending a static, large block of examples.

items.title: Use Stop Sequences
items.description: Define specific stop sequences to terminate generation as soon as the relevant data is produced, avoiding trailing filler tokens.

items.title: Truncate Chat History
items.description: Implement a sliding window or summarization strategy for conversation history to keep context windows small and manageable.

items.title: Tag Requests with Metadata
items.description: Attach user_id, feature_id, and environment tags to every API call for granular cost attribution.

items.title: Set Automated Budget Alerts
items.description: Configure provider-level and proxy-level alerts at 50%, 80%, and 100% of the monthly budget.

items.title: Implement Circuit Breakers
items.description: Code automated triggers that disable high-cost features if a specific user or feature exceeds a daily spend threshold.

items.title: Calculate Unit Economics
items.description: Establish a metric for cost-per-successful-interaction to identify features that are economically unviable.

items.title: Audit Token-to-Word Ratios
items.description: Monitor the efficiency of your tokenizer; identify if specific languages or formats are inflating token counts unexpectedly.

items.title: Migrate Async Tasks to Batch APIs
items.description: Use OpenAI or Anthropic Batch APIs for non-real-time tasks to receive a 50% discount on token pricing.

items.title: Implement Client-Side Validation
items.description: Use Pydantic or Zod to validate inputs before sending to the LLM, preventing costs on malformed requests.

items.title: Use Structured Output Modes
items.description: Force JSON mode or tool calling to reduce the need for retries caused by parsing errors.

items.title: Pre-Calculate RAG Embeddings
items.description: Ensure embeddings for static knowledge bases are calculated once during ingestion, never during the query runtime.

items.title: Evaluate Fine-Tuning for Compression
items.description: Test if a fine-tuned smaller model can match the performance of a large model with a long complex prompt.
